elements.text: Book rooms with locals, rather than hotels.

items.text: Price is an important concern for customers booking travel online.
Hotels leave you disconnected from the city and its culture.
No easy way exists to book a room with a local or become a host.

elements.text: Travelers are already solving this problem with inferior tools. The market is proven — the infrastructure is not.
Travelers already willing to stay with strangers — with zero financial incentive for hosts. Demand is there; the business model is missing.
Active supply posted through a tool with no booking, no trust layer, and no transaction infrastructure whatsoever.
Sources: Couchsurfing.com public stats; Craigslist US temporary housing category — 2008
A web platform where users rent out their space to host travelers — creating value on both sides of every transaction.
Travelers access affordable, local alternatives to hotels at a fraction of the standard nightly rate.
Hosts earn income from unused space. The first platform to give hosts a financial reason to participate.
Every booking creates a genuine local connection that the hotel model structurally cannot provide.
Search by city. Review host listings. Book in 3 clicks. Stay with a local.
We take a 10% commission on each completed transaction. No subscription fees. No listing fees. Revenue is earned only when a booking is made.
Charged to the traveler at checkout. Zero friction for hosts — they list once and receive payment minus our fee.
Based on $80/night average over 3 nights = $240 booking value. 10% = $24, rounded to $25 blended average.
At 84 million trips × $25 average fee across the serviceable market within 3 years of launch.
Internal model: 84M trips × $25 avg commission = $2.1B. See market size slide for trip count assumptions.
Starting from worldwide trip volume, we narrow to the segment we can actually win.
Total global market for accommodation bookings — all price tiers, all channels.
Travelers who book online and are price-sensitive — the segment that has a reason to switch.
15% of the available market — consistent with Couchsurfing's penetration of the same audience.
Sources: World Tourism Organization, 2007; Phocuswright online travel research, 2007; Couchsurfing user penetration model
Three channels that reach budget-conscious travelers where they are already concentrated.
Target high-density events with a listing widget

Octoberfest (6M attendees), CeBIT (700K), Summerfest (1M), Euro Cup (3M+), Mardi Gras (800K). Hotel capacity collapses at these events — we deploy the widget 8 weeks prior to capture demand at its peak.
GoLoCo, KAYAK, Orbitz — distribution partnerships that embed our inventory inside platforms travelers are already using to find budget options.
Hosts who post on Craigslist can cross-list on AirBed&Breakfast in one click — converting existing supply with zero acquisition cost.
Two dimensions define the market: price and whether transactions happen online. One quadrant is empty.
No competitor offers a transaction-based, peer-to-peer temporary housing marketplace. We define the category.
Hosts earn money — unlike Couchsurfing. Financial motivation converts passive inventory into active supply.
One persistent listing vs. Craigslist's daily re-posting. Lower host effort means more active, better-maintained listings.
Search by price, location, and check-in/check-out dates in a single interface. No email chains, no phone calls.
Browse host profiles with photos and reviews. Book in 3 clicks. Trust is built into the product, not appended.
Memorable name. Launching at the DNC — a high-density event that creates national share of mind from day one.

items.text: –Entrepreneur and product designer; holds a patent for CritBuns
–RISD graduate — dual BFAs in graphic design and industrial design
–Founder, Brian Chesky Inc — industrial design consultancy
–Harvard CS graduate; worked at Microsoft, OPNET Technologies, Batiq
–CEO & co-founder of justin.tv — live video platform with millions of viewers
–Advising on product-market fit and consumer growth strategy

elements.text: Independent coverage from technology media — unsolicited, third-party validation of the concept.
AirBed & Breakfast is a fun approach to couch surfing.
Think of it as Craigslist meets Hotels.com, but a lot less creepy.
A cool alternative to a boring evening in a hotel room.
AirBed's fee-based service could help alleviate concerns about quality of accommodations.
Real guests and hosts — unprompted, on their own experience with the platform.
I found something in my price-range, and that's what really enabled me to come to the conference.
A complete success. It is easy to use and it made me money.
It's about the ideas, the interactions, the people. You don't get that in a hotel room.
We are raising a $500K angel round to fund 12 months of operations and reach 80,000 transactions on AirBed&Breakfast.
12 months of runway to reach the first major transaction milestone and validate the unit economics at scale.
The milestone that proves market adoption and unlocks the next round. 80,000 completed bookings at $25 average fee.
80K trips × $25 average commission. Demonstrates a repeatable, scalable revenue model before Series A.
